message:
fix(demo): keep editor non-empty on retry failure + enforce const on every addClass

- main.ts tracks bestAttemptCode (the longest code any attempt
  produced during the run). After the attempt loop exits with all
  attempts failed, if the editor contents are shorter than the best
  attempt (e.g. attempt 1 produced 689ch with one fixable typo,
  attempt 2 produced 18ch and wiped the editor) we restore the best
  one so the user has the most context to hand-edit.

- class.ts: add explicit rule that EVERY addClass call must be
  assigned to a const. The previous run showed the model declaring
  Payment + CreditCard with const + connecting them, then switching
  to bare 'PayPal = addClass(...)' from the 3rd class onward,
  triggering 'PayPal is not defined' at the first connect. Sited
  clearly at rule #1.

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1 @@
-c53d67a1bc6ca86b
\ No newline at end of file
+89e7e486fc34c272
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -57,6 +57,7 @@ connect(cat, animal, "extends", { relation: "inheritance" });
 connect(owner, dog, "owns", { relation: "composition" });
 
 Rules:
+- EVERY addClass call MUST be assigned to a const — on EVERY class, not just the first few. Writing bare "PayPal = addClass(...)" without "const" fails with "PayPal is not defined" at the next connect that references it.
 - Attributes and methods ALWAYS go inside the addClass call's { attributes, methods } object. NEVER as separate connect() calls — connect is ONLY for class-to-class relationships.
 - Every relationship connect MUST include { relation: "..." } with a valid value. Unrelated edges are invalid.
 - Inheritance arrows: connect(child, parent, "extends", { relation: "inheritance" }). Direction is child → parent.
